---
title: "Proof of Gödel completeness"
---

::: {.proof of="thm-main-godel-completeness"}
Fix a first-order signature \(L\), a set \(\Gamma\) of \(L\)-sentences, and
an \(L\)-sentence \(\varphi\), exactly as in the protected main statement.
Assume \(\Gamma\models_L\varphi\) in the sense of @def-satisfaction.

Suppose for contradiction that \(\Gamma\nvdash_L\varphi\) in the calculus
@def-hilbert-calculus.  By @lem-consistency-characterizations, the set
\(\Sigma=\Gamma\cup\{\neg\varphi\}\) is syntactically consistent.
@thm-consistent-model therefore supplies an \(L\)-structure
\(\mathcal M\) satisfying every member of \(\Sigma\).  In particular,
\(\mathcal M\models\Gamma\) and
\(\mathcal M\models\neg\varphi\).  By the negation clause of
@def-satisfaction, \(\mathcal M\not\models\varphi\), contradicting
\(\Gamma\models_L\varphi\).  Hence
\(\Gamma\vdash_L\varphi\), as required.
:::
